hardware.description: Generic Giga Computing / Gigabyte server with AMI MegaRAC BMC (fallback when specific model not found)

notes: Giga Computing is the enterprise/server brand of Gigabyte
BMC firmware is AMI MegaRAC (not Gigabyte proprietary)
OEM namespace 'Ami' provides AMI-specific extensions (Configurations, MappingOut, VirtualMedia)
OEM namespace 'Gbt' provides Gigabyte hardware-level extensions
OEM namespace 'Gct' provides Giga Computing extensions (Firewall, FrontPanelSettings, NetworkBond)
RtpVersion field in Ami OEM indicates the AMI firmware build version
Manager ID is 'Self' (not numeric like Supermicro or named like Dell iDRAC)

unauthenticated_endpoints: /redfish/v1/ (service root with OEM data, UUID, MAC address)
redfish_api_notes.auth_behavior: All endpoints except service root and $metadata return Base.1.12.AccessDenied without valid session/basic auth
redfish_api_notes.screenshot_method: KVMIP supported; no DumpService or OEM screenshot endpoint found — may require HTML5 KVM or IPMI

notes: Giga Computing GPU servers typically require Above4G Decoding enabled
SR-IOV and ACS settings may vary by model and BMC firmware version
Check specific model documentation for optimal BIOS settings

research_notes.doc_discovery_method: Redfish API probe on IREN B200 nodes
notes: Giga Computing is the server/enterprise division of Gigabyte
Redfish reports manufacturer as 'Giga Computing', not 'Gigabyte'
OEM keys in service root are 'Ami' and 'Gbt' (sometimes also 'Gct' on manager)
detect_vendor() matches on Gbt/Ami OEM keys or 'ami' in Product string
